description_long: This is a large, intricately designed wooden chapel reminiscent of the historic stave churches of Scandinavia, particularly Norway. The structure features multiple steeply pitched, overlapping gabled roofs, each adorned with wooden shingles, and several tall, pointed spires that rise dramatically from the central core. The exterior walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, giving the building a warm, natural appearance. Small annexes and protruding sections surround the main body, creating a complex, multi-faceted footprint. The chapel sits on a slightly raised, irregular stone or earthen base, with a path leading up to it and some sparse landscaping around the perimeter. The overall impression is one of medieval craftsmanship, with a focus on verticality and layered rooflines.
description: A large, ornate wooden chapel with steep, shingled roofs, multiple spires, and a complex, multi-gabled structure reminiscent of a Norwegian stave church.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the main entrance, central spire, and the layered, gabled roof structure with visible annexes and a path leading up to the chapel.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the length of the chapel, showing the extended side annexes and the intricate arrangement of overlapping roofs.
description_view_2: The rear three-quarter view showcases the rounded apse, multiple spires, and the dense clustering of roof sections at the back of the chapel.
description_view_3: This opposite side view reveals the darker, shadowed side of the chapel, with several pointed spires and the complex roof geometry more pronounced.
attribute_steeple_or_bell_tower: The chapel features a prominent central steeple rising above the main roof, flanked by several smaller spires. The steeple is tall, pointed, and covered in wooden shingles, characteristic of stave church architecture.
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance door is rectangular, made of heavy wood, and likely reinforced with iron hardware. It is set within a modest wooden frame, with minimal decorative elements and a natural wood finish.

license_info.creator_display_name: Kulturarv i Vestfold og Telemark fylkeskommune
